data.title: Logan Paul's rare Pikachu card will be auctioned in 2026; Polymarket traders bet 90% above $4 million. Paul bought the card for a record $5.3 million in 2022 and encourages young investors to consider alternative assets like collectibles.
data.description: Social media personality turned businessperson Logan Paul is once again drawing attention to the market for Pokémon cards. These cards are not only seen as valuable collectibles, but also as potential investments with real market implications. During an appearance on Fox Business's "The Big Money Show," Paul showcased his ultra-rare PSA 10 Pikachu Illustrator card. This card was acquired for a groundbreaking $5.3 million in 2022. Paul encouraged young investors to explore non-traditional assets like collectibles, trading cards, and memorabilia, suggesting that taking calculated risks, especially at a young age, can yield significant returns. The card, which is one of only a few dozen in existence globally, received a perfect 10/10 grade from Professional Sports Authenticator (PSA). To emphasize its value, Paul flaunted the card mounted on a custom necklace adorned with diamonds worth $75,000. For further details, the full article is accessible on Benzinga.com.
Silver climbs 5%, touching an unprecedented $75.62 per ounce.

data.title: Arthur Hayes predicts Bitcoin could hit $750,000 by 2027 due to Trump administration's money printing.
data.description: Arthur Hayes, co-founder of BitMEX cryptocurrency exchange and Chief Investment Officer at Maelstrom Fund, made a bold prediction stating that Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) could potentially reach $750,000 by the end of 2027. In an interview on CoinDesk, Hayes suggested that the upcoming years, particularly 2026-27, will witness significant money printing which may boost Bitcoin's value to a range between $500,000 and $750,000. He attributed this potential surge to the inclination of governments, including the Trump administration, to resort to money printing as a solution during uncertain times. Hayes' forecast has stirred discussions about the impact of government interventions on the success of Bitcoin. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.
Authorities investigating after a mosque in central Syria’s Homs province is hit by an explosion, state media says.

data.title: AI sector correction in 2026 may impact Bitcoin price, with forecasts suggesting BTC could fall to $65,000 - Tether CEO.
data.description: The CEO of Tether, Paolo Ardoino, has cautioned that an upcoming correction in the AI sector in 2026 may have repercussions on the cryptocurrency markets. Analysts are predicting that Bitcoin (BTC) could potentially decrease in value, potentially dropping as low as $65,000.
Talks with Cambodia on a potential ceasefire set for Saturday, Thai Defence Minister to attend, PM confirms.

data.title: US stock futures fell after Christmas, with Dow Jones -0.12%, S&P 500 -0.06%. Initial jobless claims at 214,000. Nvidia up 0.58%.
data.description: U.S. stock futures dropped after Wednesday's higher close, with major benchmark indices sliding. The NYSE closed early Wednesday and remained shut Thursday for Christmas. U.S. initial jobless claims decreased by 10,000 to 214,000 in the week ending Dec. 20, beating estimates. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.15%, and the two-year bond was at 3.51%. CME's FedWatch predicted an 84.5% chance of the Federal Reserve maintaining interest rates in January. Futures saw changes with Dow Jones -0.12%, S&P 500 -0.06%, Nasdaq 100 -0.06%, Russell 2000 -0.27%. SPY was down 0.029% at $690.18, while QQQ declined 0.014% to $623.84 in premarket trading. Nvidia's shares rose 0.58% after announcing a licensing agreement with Groq, a startup. Dynavax Technologies soared 38.19%. NVDA maintains positive price trends, DVAX saw significant increase.

data.title: Anycubic opens deposits for Kobra X, an entry-level multicolor FDM printer, following Formnext debut.
data.description: Anycubic, a company based in Shenzhen, China, introduced its latest product, the Kobra X, at Formnext 2025. This new entry-level FDM 3D printer is aimed at making multi-color and multi-material printing easier for a wider audience. Following its showcase at the event, Anycubic has now launched a deposit program for the Kobra X. This initiative is part of their strategy to increase accessibility to advanced printing technology.

data.title: Team Infinix wins "Chicken Dinner Victory Squad" at PMGC 2025, showcasing elite gaming skills. #CrossBorderUnity. 93 characters
data.description: In a display of international teamwork and exceptional gaming prowess, the Infinix GT 30 Pro showcased its elite performance at the 2025 PUBG MOBILE SUPERSTARS UNITED event in Bangkok. Team Infinix, comprising top content creators from around the world, impressed with their skills during the highly competitive event on December 26, 2025. The cross-border collaboration was truly highlighted as they demonstrated their gaming abilities to a global audience, achieving a commendable result.

data.title: A.I. firms borrowing billions face high interest rates as debt investors turn cautious.
data.description: Debt investors are asking for high interest rates from artificial intelligence companies seeking to secure funds, as caution grows among investors. This trend highlights the increasing challenges faced by AI firms in raising capital in the current market environment.

data.title: Tech giants invest billions in southern Indian megacities to build AI infrastructure for data-hungry India.
data.description: Large cities in the southern region of India are receiving significant investments to develop artificial intelligence infrastructure. This investment aims to cater to the high data demand in the country, which is one of the world's largest in terms of data consumption.

data.title: Tech workers continue to challenge companies despite crackdowns on activism. They view themselves as regular employees with common concerns.
data.description: They are starting to view themselves more as ordinary employees who have common complaints about their employers.

data.title: Various celebrities, including a "Shark Tank" investor and NBA All-Stars, cameo in 'Marty Supreme,' a table tennis comedy.
data.description: A well-known investor from the popular show "Shark Tank," a prominent supermarket owner, and even basketball All-Stars from the N.B.A. are featured in the comedy centered around table tennis.

data.title: In Lugano, Switzerland, Bitcoin is widely accepted for payments, from McDonald’s to taxes, showcasing current infrastructure readiness.
data.description: Lugano is exemplifying Bitcoin adoption through various avenues, including partnerships with McDonald’s and the implementation of Bitcoin payments for municipal taxes. The city's proactive approach highlights the importance of developing the necessary infrastructure to accommodate the increasing use of Bitcoin in the present, rather than simply trying to forecast its future implications. The diversified efforts in Lugano underscore the growing influence of Bitcoin, showcasing its relevance in everyday transactions. This strategy emphasizes the city's commitment to embracing digital currencies and preparing for a future where Bitcoin plays a prevalent role.

data.title: In 2025, students and teachers from US states participated in a cultural exchange in China to learn about Eastern culture.
data.description: In December 26, 2025, in SHIJIAZHUANG, China, a notable News Report from Great Wall New Media revealed that numerous middle school teachers and students from various states in the United States embarked on a trip to China. This journey was motivated by their curiosity and excitement to explore Eastern civilization. The group consisted of a significant number of participants, pointing towards the widespread interest in experiencing a different culture.

data.title: Smartee hosts European Children's Orthodontic Summit at Disneyland Paris, bringing together 52 orthodontists from 8 countries.
data.description: In Paris on December 18, 2025, the European Children's Orthodontic Summit was held by Smartee Denti-Technology at Disneyland® Paris. The event united 52 orthodontists from various countries including France, Switzerland, Belgium, the Netherlands, Spain, Algeria, and Tunisia.
